title: Signed value used for bit select gives unexpected behavior.

features.name: signed wire as bit-select index
features.description: Code should include a signed wire used as the index in a bit-select assignment, where the signed wire holds a non-negative value obtained by zero-extending a narrower unsigned input

features.name: concatenation zero-extension into signed wire
features.description: Code should include a signed wire initialized by concatenating a zero literal with a smaller unsigned input, effectively zero-extending it while keeping a signed type
features.snippet:
input [1:0] i;
wire signed [3:0] idx = {2'b00, i};

features.name: combinational bit-select assignment for one-hot encoding
features.description: Code should include a combinational always block that clears a register and then sets a single bit selected by a dynamic index, implementing a one-hot decoder
